text:
In the quiet town of Whispering Pines, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He loved nothing more than spending his days listening to the whispers of nature. One crisp autumn evening, Oliver decided it was time to tell his favorite granddaughter tales for her bed.

"Once upon a time," Oliver began, nestled comfortably in his nest among leaves, "there was a pond filled with crystal-clear water. Its surface glittered like gems under the moonlight."

He paused to let his words sink in.

"The wildlife of Whispering Pines adored it. Ducks glided gracefully among the reeds, while frogs croaked in harmony. Even the rabbits liked to soak their little hooves in its gentle embrace."

Oliver chuckled softly.

"But one peculiar creature lived in the pond—a goose named Gertrude. Gertrude wasn't like the others. She didn't swim. Instead, she preferred to float. And she had a quirky rule—never nibble upon the floating mats."
